headline: Malaysia's 2024 Deficit 4.1%, 2025 Came In at 3.7%; PFFRA Targets Frame the Glide Path
context: Malaysia's 2024 fiscal deficit narrowed to 4.1% of GDP, better than the 4.3% original target. MOF reported the 2025 outturn at 3.7%, below the 3.8% projection. The Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 sets a medium-term 3%-of-GDP deficit target.

cards.big: Malaysia narrowed its deficit to 4.1% in 2024 and 3.7% in 2025, beating the 3.8% projection. The PFFRA medium-term target is 3% of GDP.
cards.sub: Analyst forecasts had flagged risks from weaker tax revenue, lower petroleum revenue and SST-broadening delays. Final outturn came in below those forecasts.

cards.big: Every 1 percentage point of deficit adds tens of billions to annual borrowing.
cards.sub: Long-term sustainability requires revenue mobilisation (broader SST, CGT) or sustained spending discipline. Both involve political trade-offs.

cards.big: Credit rating outlooks track consistent target performance. Downgrades are associated with higher yields and risk premia across the sovereign curve.
cards.sub: The pass-through to corporate bond markets is well documented; specific mortgage and household-credit channels depend on local market conditions.

cards.big: The Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 (Act 850) provides statutory fiscal objectives and a 3-to-5-year MTFF.
cards.sub: It sets a 3%-of-GDP medium-term deficit target and requires the Finance Minister to lay an annual fiscal-outlook statement before Parliament.

cards.big: The 2025 target was met. The open question is whether the glide path holds to the 3% PFFRA target without revenue or spending action.
Like a household that hit its annual savings target. The trend is right; the question is whether next year stays on it.
cards.sub: Hitting one target does not establish a glide path. Markets price the trajectory, not the single year.

cards.big: PFFRA already provides the statutory frame. The next test is enforcement: whether the fiscal-adjustment mechanism is triggered if the glide path slips.
